description: Nezha dashboard lets RoleMember users create cron tasks through commonHandler and bypass per-server permission checks with Cover=CronCoverAll and an empty Servers list. CronTrigger then fans attacker-controlled commands out to every monitored server and exfiltrates output through notifications.

vulns.description: Cron write and trigger routes are wired through commonHandler instead of adminHandler, so any authenticated RoleMember can reach cron creation/update/manual trigger APIs.
createCron checks ServerShared.CheckPermission over cf.Servers; an empty server list iterates zero items and returns true, bypassing per-server permissions.
CronTrigger iterates every server in global ServerShared and sends TaskTypeCommand with cr.Command without checking that the cron owner may control each server.
Task command output result.GetData is sent to the cron NotificationGroup, allowing attacker-controlled webhook exfiltration when PushSuccessful is true.
